### Shell Completion

Prospero provides shell completion support for bash and zsh shells. Shell completion allows you to use the Tab key to auto-complete commands, options, and arguments.

#### Installing bash completion

To enable bash completion for the current shell session:

[source, bash]
----
$ source <(./prospero.sh completion)
----

To make completion permanent, add the completion script to your bash profile:

[source, bash]
----
$ ./prospero.sh completion >> ~/.bashrc
----

Or on macOS:

[source, bash]
----
$ ./prospero.sh completion >> ~/.bash_profile
----

#### Installing zsh completion

For zsh, the completion script can also be generated and loaded:

[source, bash]
----
$ source <(./prospero.sh completion)
----

To make it permanent for zsh:

[source, bash]
----
$ ./prospero.sh completion > "${fpath[1]}/_prospero"
----

Then restart your shell or run:

[source, bash]
----
$ autoload -U compinit && compinit
----

#### Using completion

Once enabled, you can use Tab to complete Prospero commands:

[source, bash]
----
$ ./prospero.sh up<TAB>
# Completes to: ./prospero.sh update

$ ./prospero.sh update <TAB>
# Shows: apply  list  perform  prepare  subscribe  list-manifest-versions
----

Completion also works for command options and can complete file paths and directory names where appropriate.
